Content Direction by Mase · Monthly membership

Make better content for your business—with someone to guide you.

You do not need to buy everything or learn every editing program. You need the right setup, a workable process and honest guidance about what will make the biggest difference.

Register your interestSee how it works

Pricing coming soon

01 · Choose well02 · Make with purpose03 · Improve each month

Who it is for

A practical route into creating your own business content.

Content Direction is for small-business owners and aspiring content creators who want to make more of their own photography, video and graphics—but want experienced direction before spending money or losing hours to trial and error.

It is not a one-off course and it is not a substitute for professional production. It is ongoing guidance for the content your business can sensibly create in-house, with a clear route to Mase when a brief needs a full creative production.

Small-business ownersSole tradersIn-house beginnersAspiring creators

You might be in the right place if…

The problem is not effort.
It is knowing what matters.

01

You are not sure whether your phone is enough or a camera would genuinely help.

02

Every gear video adds something else to a shopping list you do not trust.

03

You can capture content, but editing it takes too long or feels inconsistent.

04

You only decide what to post when the business is already busy.

05

You have tried several apps and still do not have a simple workflow.

06

You want your content to improve without pretending every job should be done in-house.

What the membership gives you

Direction you can put to work.

The focus stays on practical decisions for your business—not collecting more theory.

01

Better buying decisions

Guidance on cameras, phones, lighting, audio and supporting gear based on your real needs and budget.

02

Sensible software choices

Help choosing photo, video and graphic-design software that fits the way you work.

03

A workable production process

A clearer route from planning and capture through to editing, exporting and using the finished content.

04

Useful monthly direction

Help thinking through what your business should make next, so content is connected to real priorities.

05

More consistent output

Practical guidance on improving quality without making the process too complicated to repeat.

06

Honest production advice

Clarity on what you can create well yourself and when professional photography or film would earn its place.

Guidance areas

From the first purchase
to the final export.

Advice stays grounded in the content your business actually needs to make.

01

Gear

Choose cameras, phones, lighting, audio and supporting kit around the work you actually need to make—not somebody else’s shopping list.

02

Photography

Get clearer on light, framing and simple repeatable setups so your everyday business photographs feel more intentional.

03

Video

Plan and capture useful footage with steadier pictures, clearer sound and a purpose before you press record.

04

Editing

Select sensible photo and video software, then shape a workflow that takes you from files on a device to a finished export.

05

Posters & graphics

Choose suitable design tools and make clearer promotional graphics with better hierarchy, sizing and consistency.

Why monthly

Your content needs will move.
Your guidance should too.

This is ongoing practical support, not a library of lessons to finish once and forget.

  1. 01

    Start with where you are

    Your experience, existing equipment, budget and business goals give the guidance its context.

  2. 02

    Focus on the next useful improvement

    Work on the decisions that matter now instead of trying to master every tool and format at once.

  3. 03

    Keep the process relevant

    As your content, confidence and business priorities change, the direction can change with them.

The exact membership format, payment and subscription details are being finalised. Pricing and terms will be shared before you are asked to subscribe.

Two useful ways to work

Make it yourself.
Bring in Mase when it counts.

Good content does not have to come from one route. Choose the level of support the brief deserves.

Create it yourself · With guidance

Best for regular, everyday content.

Choose Content Direction when you want to build your own skills, make smart use of a realistic kit budget and create repeatable content inside the business.

  • Day-to-day social content
  • Simple product, people and behind-the-scenes updates
  • Building confidence and a consistent process
Register your interest

Professional production · By Mase

Best when the finish and the moment carry more weight.

Bring in Mase for higher-quality production, campaigns, launches or ongoing creative support when the business needs stronger ideas, specialist execution or time back.

  • Campaigns, launches and important moments
  • Polished photography and film
  • Ongoing monthly content produced for you
Explore professional services

The two can work together: keep everyday content moving in-house, then use professional production to raise the level when the opportunity calls for it.

Frequently asked questions

Straight answers,
before you start.

Do I need content-creation experience?

No. The membership is for beginners as well as people who have already started and want a clearer process. Guidance should meet you where you are without talking down to you.

What if I already own equipment?

Start there. The aim is to make better decisions with what you have, identify genuine gaps and avoid replacing useful kit for the sake of it.

Will every recommendation be expensive?

No. Recommendations should reflect your budget, business needs and the content you need to produce. Sometimes the most useful change is how you use your current setup.

Which editing or design software will you recommend?

That depends on the work you make, the device you use, your experience and your budget. The point is to choose suitable software—not push everybody towards the same app.

Can I cancel the membership?

Cancellation and payment terms are still being finalised. They will be made clear before anybody subscribes; you can register your interest now and ask Mason for the confirmed details when they are ready.
